KEARNY COUNTY HOSPITAL LTCU

CCN: 17E531 · LAKIN, KS 67860 · Kearny County

Overview

Address
607 COURT PL, LAKIN, KS 67860
Phone
6203557836

Avg daily residents

Provider type

Setting
Rural
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
Footnote 23.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 2/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 2/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures — Data not available.

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2024-10-24 33 33 0 328 1 328
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2022-12-07 7 7 0 52 1 52
Total weighted health score
259.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (43)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

1 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
40 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
1 G/H/I Actual harm
1 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2024-10-24 2024-12-06
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. E 2024-10-24 2024-12-06
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2024-10-24 2024-12-06
0623 Provide timely notification to the resident, and if applicable to the resident representative and ombudsman, before transfer or discharge, including appeal rights. E 2024-10-24 2024-12-06
0625 Notify the resident or the resident’s representative in writing how long the nursing home will hold the resident’s bed in cases of transfer to a hospital or therapeutic leave. E 2024-10-24 2024-12-06
0636 Assess the resident completely in a timely manner when first admitted, and then periodically, at least every 12 months. F 2024-10-24 2024-12-06
0638 Assure that each resident’s assessment is updated at least once every 3 months. E 2024-10-24 2024-12-06
0640 Encode each resident’s assessment data and transmit these data to the State within 7 days of assessment. E 2024-10-24 2024-12-06
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. F 2024-10-24 2024-12-06
0655 Create and put into place a plan for meeting the resident's most immediate needs within 48 hours of being admitted D 2024-10-24 2024-12-06
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-10-24 2024-12-06
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. E 2024-10-24 2024-12-06
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. G 2024-10-24 2024-12-06
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2024-10-24 2024-12-06
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. E 2024-10-24 2024-12-06
0712 Ensure that the resident and his/her doctor meet face-to-face at all required visits. D 2024-10-24 2024-12-06
0727 Have a registered nurse on duty 8 hours a day; and select a registered nurse to be the director of nurses on a full time basis. F 2024-10-24 2024-12-06
0732 Post nurse staffing information every day. C 2024-10-24 2024-12-06
0744 Provide the appropriate treatment and services to a resident who displays or is diagnosed with dementia. D 2024-10-24 2024-12-06
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2024-10-24 2024-12-06
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2024-10-24 2024-12-06
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2024-10-24 2024-12-06
0806 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food that accommodates resident allergies, intolerances, and preferences, as well as appealing options. D 2024-10-24 2024-12-06
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2024-10-24 2024-12-06
0814 Dispose of garbage and refuse properly. F 2024-10-24 2024-12-06
0835 Administer the facility in a manner that enables it to use its resources effectively and efficiently. F 2024-10-24 2024-12-06
0851 Electronically submit to CMS complete and accurate direct care staffing information, based on payroll and other verifiable and auditable data. F 2024-10-24 2024-12-06
0867 Set up an ongoing quality assessment and assurance group to review quality deficiencies and develop corrective plans of action. F 2024-10-24 2024-12-06
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. F 2024-10-24 2024-12-06
0881 Implement a program that monitors antibiotic use. F 2024-10-24 2024-12-06
0882 Designate a qualified infection preventionist to be responsible for the infection prevent and control program in the nursing home. F 2024-10-24 2024-12-06
0883 Develop and implement policies and procedures for flu and pneumonia vaccinations. E 2024-10-24 2024-12-06
0947 Ensure nurse aides have the skills they need to care for residents, and give nurse aides education in dementia care and abuse prevention. F 2024-10-24 2024-12-06
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2022-12-07 2023-01-11
0688 Provide appropriate care for a resident to maintain and/or improve range of motion (ROM), limited ROM and/or mobility, unless a decline is for a medical reason. D 2022-12-07 2023-01-11
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2022-12-07 2023-01-11
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2022-12-07 2023-01-11
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2022-12-07 2023-01-11
0867 Set up an ongoing quality assessment and assurance group to review quality deficiencies and develop corrective plans of action. F 2022-12-07 2023-01-11
0868 Have the Quality Assessment and Assurance group have the required members and meet at least quarterly F 2022-12-07 2023-01-11
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2021-05-27 2021-06-02
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. K 2021-05-27 2021-06-04
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2021-05-27 2021-06-07
